display.purpose: Hero statements and page-opening emotional register only.

headline.purpose: Major section statements and closing calls to action.

title.purpose: Cards, grouped ideas, linked detail titles, and nested headings.

body.purpose: Readable practical copy, form content, and explanatory paragraphs.

label.purpose: Short eyebrows, captions, metadata, and low-priority form support.

shadows.name: ambient-low
shadows.purpose: Shared restrained shadow for panels, menus, forms, and fee cards.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Light separation for reusable cards and copy panels.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Button, navigation, and simple state changes.

motion.name: card-hover
motion.purpose: Interactive card transform, border, and shadow changes.

motion.name: faq-ease
motion.purpose: FAQ grid-row expansion and answer reveal.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Cedar action button for the main enquiry or next step.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; border: 1px solid var(--cedar, #234b3d); border-radius: var(--radius, 8px); padding: 0.78rem 1.08rem; background: var(--cedar, #234b3d); color: var(--surface-strong, #fcfcfa); box-shadow: 0 8px 18px rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.14); font: inherit; font-weight: 650; line-height: 1.2; cursor: pointer; transition: background 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease, color 160ms ease, transform 160ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--cedar-dark, #1d4034); border-color: var(--cedar-dark, #1d4034); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.45); outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet framed action for secondary paths.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; border: 1px solid var(--line, #ccd4ca); border-radius: var(--radius, 8px); padding: 0.78rem 1.08rem; background: var(--surface-strong, #fcfcfa); color: var(--ink, #1f231f); font: inherit; font-weight: 650; line-height: 1.2; cursor: pointer; transition: background 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease, color 160ms ease, transform 160ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--paper, #f7f6f2); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.45); outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Tertiary Button
components.description: Soft cedar action for low-pressure navigation.
components.css: .ds-btn-tertiary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 9px; border: 1px solid var(--line, #ccd4ca); border-radius: var(--radius, 8px); padding: 0.78rem 1.08rem; background: var(--cedar-soft, #eef2ec); color: var(--cedar, #234b3d); font: inherit; font-weight: 650; line-height: 1.2; cursor: pointer; transition: background 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease, color 160ms ease, transform 160ms ease; } .ds-btn-tertiary:hover { background: var(--surface, #eef2ec); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-tertiary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.45); outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Site Card
components.description: Primary card shell for service themes and grouped practical content.
components.html:
Shame and self-worth

Harsh self-judgement, a persistent sense of not being enough, or something hard to say plainly.
components.css: .ds-site-card { display: grid; gap: 18px; padding: 28px; border: 1px solid var(--line, #ccd4ca); border-radius: 10px; background: var(--surface-strong, #fcfcfa); box-shadow: 0 14px 30px rgba(31, 35, 31, 0.045); transition: transform 180ms ease, border-color 180ms ease, box-shadow 180ms ease; } .ds-site-card:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); border-color: var(--cedar, #234b3d); box-shadow: 0 18px 36px rgba(31, 35, 31, 0.075); } .ds-site-card h3 { margin: 0; color: var(--ink, #1f231f); font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif; font-size: 1.45rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.18; } .ds-site-card p { margin: 0; color: var(--muted, #505a51); font-size: 1.02rem; line-height: 1.58; }

components.name: Form Field
components.description: Clean enquiry-form field with cedar focus state.
components.css: .ds-form-row { display: grid; gap: 8px; color: var(--ink, #1f231f); font-weight: 700; } .ds-form-row input { width: 100%; min-height: 50px; border: 1px solid var(--line, #ccd4ca); border-radius: var(--radius, 8px); background: var(--surface-strong, #fcfcfa); color: var(--ink, #1f231f); padding: 13px 14px; font: inherit; outline: none; transition: border-color 160ms ease, box-shadow 160ms ease; } .ds-form-row input:focus { border-color: var(--cedar, #234b3d); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.12); }

components.name: Radio Choice
components.description: Progressive enquiry choice with clear selected and focus states.
components.css: .ds-choice { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; min-height: 50px; border: 1px solid var(--cedar, #234b3d); border-radius: var(--radius, 8px); background: var(--cedar-soft, #eef2ec); padding: 13px 14px; color: var(--ink, #1f231f); font: inherit; font-weight: 650; line-height: 1.35; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 160ms ease, background-color 160ms ease, box-shadow 160ms ease; } .ds-choice input { margin: 0.2em 0 0; accent-color: var(--cedar, #234b3d); } .ds-choice:focus-within { border-color: var(--cedar, #234b3d); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.12); }

components.name: FAQ Item
components.description: Calm accordion item with rounded paper surface and cedar icon.
components.html: A first enquiry can be brief. The work can begin from there.
components.css: .ds-faq-item { overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid rgba(204, 212, 202, 0.92); border-radius: 16px; background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(252, 252, 249, 0.96), rgba(247, 246, 242, 0.98)); transition: border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1), background 220ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1); } .ds-faq-question { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) 36px; gap: 16px; align-items: center; width: 100%; padding: 14px 22px 14px 26px; border: 0; background: transparent; color: var(--ink, #1f231f); text-align: left; cursor: pointer; font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif; font-size: 1.34rem; line-height: 1.18; } .ds-faq-icon { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 36px; height: 36px; border: 1px solid rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.16); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.04); color: var(--cedar, #234b3d); } .ds-faq-item p { margin: 0; padding: 0 26px 24px; color: var(--body, #3f493f); font-size: 1.02rem; line-height: 1.66; } .ds-faq-question:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.45); outline-offset: 4px; border-radius: 12px; }

components.name: Hero Copy Panel
components.description: Ruled copy rail for page-opening hero support.
components.html: Online counselling for adults across Australia. Direct, thoughtful, and non-shaming.
components.css: .ds-hero-copy-panel { display: grid; margin: 0; padding-left: 22px; border-left: 2px solid var(--cedar, #234b3d); color: var(--body, #3f493f); font-size: 1.08rem; line-height: 1.72; } .ds-hero-copy-panel p { margin: 0; color: var(--body, #3f493f); }

components.name: Trust List
components.description: Short factual reassurance strip with dot separators.
components.html:
• Online across Australia
  • For adults
  • Grounded and non-shaming
components.css: .ds-trust-list { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px 0; max-width: 830px; margin: 0; padding: 0; color: #3f4a40; font-size: 1.02rem; font-weight: 660; line-height: 1.35; list-style: none; } .ds-trust-list li { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; } .ds-trust-list li:not(:last-child)::after { width: 4px; height: 4px; margin-inline: 12px; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(35, 75, 61, 0.56); content: ''; }

narrative.overview:
Vive should feel like a clear way into difficult material: direct, human, a little unexpected, and not embarrassed by its own point of view. The system is grounded on purpose: paper backgrounds, sage surfaces, cedar action colour, serif headings, practical body copy, visible borders, and modest depth. It should not ingratiate, over-soothe, or decorate around the work. It can still be bold, memorable, and quietly playful.

The site should carry the plain-spoken trust of Beyond Blue without copying its institutional tone. It must feel human, literate, specific, and adult, with enough bite to avoid therapy-directory sameness. The product context is explicit: Make the room feel competent before it feels comforting. Visual decisions should do the same.

This system rejects AI wellness copy, vague safe space language, pastel counselling cliches, glossy startup therapy branding, floating positivity, solemn blandness, and any treatment that makes hard material look pre-solved.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained paper-and-sage foundation with cedar used for action, emphasis, and controlled moments of bite.
Serif-led editorial hierarchy with practical sans-serif body copy.
Borders, spacing, and tonal shifts provide structure before shadow.
Components feel calm, direct, and functional, but major public pages can still have one memorable editorial move.
Inclusion is treated as ordinary competence, not badge language.

rules.name: The Cedar Rarity Rule
rules.body: Cedar is not a background system. Use it for actions, focus, links, active states, ruled emphasis, and small proof points.

rules.name: The No Wellness Pastel Rule
rules.body: Sage can be soft, but it must stay grounded by ink, borders, and serious typography.

rules.name: The Paper First Rule
rules.body: Most of the page should remain paper or near-paper. Let the writing and hierarchy do the work.

rules.name: The Controlled Rupture Rule
rules.body: A major page can break the quiet with one deliberate move: scale, asymmetry, a sharper support panel, or a stronger cedar moment. Never use rupture as decoration.

rules.name: The One Strong H1 Rule
rules.body: One page-opening H1 sets the emotional register. Do not stack competing hero statements.

rules.name: The Practical Body Rule
rules.body: Body copy is not where the brand performs. It should read cleanly, say the thing, and get out of the way.

rules.name: The Line Break Rule
rules.body: Hero and section line breaks can carry attitude. Use them deliberately instead of centering every message into a safe block.

rules.name: The Responsive Type Rule
rules.body: Type role variables compress at the 900px breakpoint. Never copy desktop display sizes into mobile-specific rules.

rules.name: The Border Before Shadow Rule
rules.body: Use borders and tonal layering first. Strong shadows make this site feel like a SaaS dashboard.

rules.name: The No Gloss Rule
rules.body: No glass, no blur, no shiny stacked cards, no decorative glow.

dos: Do reuse the shared `site-*` and `hero-*` systems before creating page-specific styling.
Do keep pages grounded in Warm Paper, Sage Wash, Clean Paper, Green Black, and Cedar Green.
Do use borders, ruled paragraphs, split layouts, and spacing to create hierarchy.
Do keep ruled accents thin and editorial unless a legacy component already owns a thicker treatment.
Do give major public pages one memorable editorial move when the subject calls for it.
Do use controlled asymmetry, strong line breaks, and sharp supporting details to keep the site authored.
Do make practical details easy to scan: fees, format, availability, contact expectations, and limits.
Do write visual examples and copy with the PRODUCT.md line in mind: Make the room feel competent before it feels comforting.
Do preserve semantic HTML, visible focus states, keyboard-friendly controls, reduced-motion care, and readable contrast.
Do treat WCAG AA as the working accessibility floor.
donts: Don't use AI wellness copy, vague safe space language, waffly therapy generalities, pastel counselling cliches, inspirational fluff, clinical coldness, glossy startup therapy branding, over-explaining, euphemism, or copy that sounds generated.
Don't make inclusion feel like badge language. Kink, ENM, polyamory, and LGBTQIA+ competence should read as ordinary practice knowledge.
Don't add floating blobs, soft-focus positivity, generic stock calm, glassmorphism, loud gradients, or glossy effects.
Don't turn public pages into app-style dashboards, SaaS/product-launch templates, or pressure-heavy sales funnels.
Don't introduce ad hoc colours when an existing token already fits.
Don't use `ds-*`, `design-language-*`, `legacy-*`, `test-bed-*`, `opus-*`, or `inc-lab-*` classes on production pages.
Don't add page-specific cards, heroes, panels, or CTA styles when a shared pattern is already close.
Don't confuse seriousness with blandness, institutional caution, or therapy-directory sameness.
Don't add new 4px coloured side stripes to cards, panels, callouts, or alerts. Use thin rules, full borders, background shifts, or typographic emphasis instead.
